title: Trigger finger - NHS
text:
al anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs) to help with pain splinting, which is where the affected finger is strapped to a plastic splint to keep it straight and reduce movement steroid injections  into your finger or thumb to reduce swelling and pain surgery to help your finger or thumb move properly (this is only done if other treatments have not worked)

Next review due: 17 November 2028
